channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Driverless 'bus of the future' is tested in Barcelona
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — Commuters in downtown Barcelona have been able to ride the bus for free this week. There’s just one catch: this mini-bus has no one at the wheel.
item.dc:creator: HERNAN MUÑOZ and JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Vance hopeful 'high-level' TikTok deal will be completed by early April
item.description: Vice President JD Vance said Friday that he was hopeful a deal to keep TikTok operating in the U.S. will be wrapped up by the early April deadline.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Human firewalls: The first line of defense against cyber threats in 2025
item.description: Now that all the industry thought leaders have weighed in and published their predictions for what the cybersecurity landscape will look like in 2025, it seems that there’s a clear consensus emerging: Both attacks and defense of devices and networks will increasingly be AI-driven as time goes by.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: UN report reveals alarming extent of Iran’s increased surveillance of women
item.description: Iran increasingly relies on electronic surveillance and the public to inform on women refusing to wear the country’s mandatory headscarf in public, even as hard-liners push for harsher penalties for those protesting the law, a United Nations report released Friday found.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: The Bay Area Adds Jobs in January Despite Statewide Slump
item.description: The Bay Area added jobs in January, bucking a statewide trend of stagnation, according to new data from the California Employment Development Department.
item.dc:creator: George Avalos, The Mercury News

item.title: The Leica D-Lux 8 is Finally Available to Buy - A Welcome Surprise in the Premium Compact Camera Market
item.description: The Leica D-Lux 8 premium compact camera is now available for purchase, bringing a welcome surprise to the market. We take a closer look at whether this updated model still has what it takes to compete with other popular compact cameras like the Fujifilm X100VI and Canon PowerShot G7X Mark III.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: IT leaders are driving a new cloud computing era
item.description: At fictional Tech Innovations Inc., a midsize software company, the rapid adoption of cloud technology was once seen as a transformative stepping stone toward agility and scalability.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Meta to start testing crowd-sourced fact-checking, based on X example, next week
item.description: acebook and Instagram parent company Meta Platforms Inc. said Thursday it will begin testing its crowd-sourced fact-checking program, Community Notes, on March 18.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, The Mercury News

item.title: Intel shares jump after naming chip industry veteran Tan CEO
item.description: Shares of Intel Corp. jumped the most in five years after the company named Lip-Bu Tan as its next chief executive officer, entrusting a former board member and semiconductor veteran with one of the toughest jobs in the chip industry.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Dangerous Chrome extensions mimic password managers
item.description: Kurt the CyberGuy explains how to protect yourself from malicious Chrome extensions that can steal sensitive information like password managers, crypto wallets and banking apps.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Asian elephant population in Cambodia is more robust than previously thought, a study finds
item.description: A genetic study of Asian elephants in northern Cambodia reveals a larger and more robust population than previously thought, raising hopes the endangered species could slowly recover.
item.dc:creator: Sopheng Cheang And David Rising, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: At long last, OpenStack (now known as OpenInfra Foundation) joins Linux Foundation
item.description: After more than a decade as an independent open-source foundation, the OpenStack project is joining the Linux Foundation in a move aimed at accelerating collaboration across the open infrastructure ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: New tools from OpenAI help companies create their own AI agents
item.description: OpenAI on Tuesday launched Responses, a new api intended to eventually replace Assistants. The big draw? Responses provides a number of new tools that companies and organizations can use to create their own AI agents.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Malware steals bank cards and passwords from millions of devices
item.description: One report claims at least 25 million people were targeted by infostealer malware between the beginning of 2023 and the end of 2024, capturing bank card details and passwords.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: French publishers and authors sue Meta over copyright works used in AI training
item.description: French publishers and authors said Wednesday they're taking Meta to court, accusing the social media company of using their works without permission to train its artificial intelligence model.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says TikTok deal is in the works. Here's where things stand with the company
item.description: In less than a month, TikTok could have one or a few new owners, be banned again, or simply receive another reprieve to continue operating in the United States.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Dangerous malware targets you through fake loan app
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says a malware app called SpyLend is posing as a financial tool, hitting 100,000-plus downloads.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 3 of the best LLM integration tools for R
item.description: When we first looked at this space in late 2023, many generative AI R packages focused largely on OpenAI and ChatGPT-like functionality or coding add-ins. Today, the landscape includes packages that natively support more LLM suppliers—including models run locally on your own computer.
item.dc:creator: Info World
